ogTitle: St Helens vs St Helens Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in St Helens and St Helens Plains.
heading: St Helens vs St Helens Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Helens, Victoria and St Helens Plains, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Helens has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to St Helens Plains (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Helens or St Helens Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Helens has a flood risk score of 44/100, while St Helens Plains scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Helens scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. St Helens Plains scores 62/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Helens or St Helens Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Helens has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
